documentation:
# module-qc-analysis-tools v2.3.0 A general python tool for running ITkPixV1.1 module QC test analysis. An overview of the steps in the module QC procedure is documented in the [Electrical specification and QC procedures for ITkPixV1.1 modules](https://gitlab.cern.ch/atlas-itk/pixel/module/itkpix-electrical-qc/) document and in [this spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qGzrCl4iD9362RwKlstZASbhphV_qTXPeBC-VSttfgE/edit#gid=989740987). ---
| | | | --- | --- | | CI/CD | [![CI - Test][cicd-badge]][cicd-link] | | Docs | [![Docs - Badge][docs-badge]][docs-link] | | Package | [![PyPI - Downloads - Total][pypi-downloads-total]][pypi-link] [![PyPI - Downloads - Per Month][pypi-downloads-dm]][pypi-link] [![PyPI - Version][pypi-version]][pypi-link] [![PyPI platforms][pypi-platforms]][pypi-link] | | Meta | [![GitLab - Issue][gitlab-issues-badge]][gitlab-issues-link] [![License - MIT][license-badge]][license-link] | [cicd-badge]: https://gitlab.cern.ch/atlas-itk/pixel/module/module-qc-analysis-tools/badges/main/pipeline.svg [cicd-link]: https://gitlab.cern.ch/atlas-itk/pixel/module/module-qc-analysis-tools/-/commits/main [docs-badge]: https://img.shields.io/badge/documentation-mkdocs-brightgreen?style=for-the-badge&logo=data:image/png;base64, [docs-link]: https://atlas-itk-pixel-mqat.docs.cern.ch [gitlab-issues-badge]: https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Issues&message=File&color=blue&logo=gitlab [gitlab-issues-link]: https://gitlab.cern.ch/atlas-itk/pixel/module/module-qc-analysis-tools/-/issues [pypi-link]: https://pypi.org/project/module-qc-analysis-tools/ [pypi-downloads-dm]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/module-qc-analysis-tools.svg?color=blue&label=Downloads&logo=pypi&logoColor=gold [pypi-downloads-total]: https://pepy.tech/badge/module-qc-analysis-tools [pypi-platforms]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/module-qc-analysis-tools [pypi-version]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/module-qc-analysis-tools [license-badge]: https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg [license-link]: https://spdx.org/licenses/MIT.html

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.QUAD_MODULE_METROLOGY.main
documentation: Performs analysis of quad module metrology.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.OVERVOLTAGE_PROTECTION.main
documentation:
Performs the Overvoltage protection analysis.

It produces an output file with the measured internal voltages and currents in low power mode and when overvoltage protection mechanism is activated.

modules.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.main
documentation: Top-level entrypoint for the command line interface.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.main.main
documentation: Manage top-level options

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.ADC_CALIBRATION.main
documentation:
Performs the ADC calibration.

It produces several diagnostic plots and an output file with the ADC calibration slope and offset.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.load_yarr_scans.main
documentation:
Given a directory with YARR scans, the script will identify the latest YARR scans needed for all analyses. Alternatively, the paths to YARR scans for each type of scan can be supplied to the script.

Before running analysis involving YARR scans (such as `MIN_HEALTH_TEST`, `TUNING`, `PIXEL_FAILURE_ANALYSIS`), the YARR scans to be analyzed need to be identified.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.MIN_HEALTH_TEST.main
documentation:
Performs the minimum health analysis of YARR Scans.

It produces an output file with key parameters (number of dead/bad pixels, ...).  Note that the YARR scans to be used in the analysis should be identified with [`mqat analysis load-yarr-scans`](#mqat-config-load-yarr-scans).

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.DATA_TRANSMISSION.main
documentation:
Performs the data transmission.

It produces several diagnostic plots and an output file with the eye diagram width.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.QUAD_BARE_MODULE_METROLOGY.main
documentation:
Performs analysis of quad bare module metrology.

The QC criteria are based on the following documents from the corresponding [EDMS entry](https://edms.cern.ch/document/1932682/12):

- https://edms.cern.ch/ui/file/1932682/12/2022-05-25_ITk_Pixel_Bare_Quad_Module_150um.pdf
- https://edms.cern.ch/ui/file/1932682/12/2022-05-25_ITk_Pixel_Bare_Quad_Module_100um.pdf

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.LP_MODE.main
documentation:
Performs the Low Power mode analysis.

It produces an output file with the measured internal voltages and currents in low power mode.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.UNDERSHUNT_PROTECTION.main
documentation:
Performs the Undershunt protection analysis.

It produces an output file with the measured internal voltages and currents in when the undershunt protection mechanism is activated.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.INJECTION_CAPACITANCE.main
documentation:
Performs the injection capacitance.

It produces several diagnostic plots and an output file with the measured pixel injection capacitance.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.PIXEL_FAILURE_ANALYSIS.main
documentation:
Classifies pixel failures and performs the pixel failure analysis.

It produces an output file with several key parameters (number of pixels failing each category, total failing pixels, ...). Note that the YARR scans to be used in the analysis should be identified with [`mqat analysis load-yarr-scans`](#mqat-config-load-yarr-scans).

---

General idea of how this analysis works:

- Pixels are flagged as either passing or failing a range of selections.
- This information is stored in a flat numpy array of 153600 16-bit integers, called 'pix_fail'
- Each bit corresponds to a single pixel failure category (i.e. DIGITAL_DEAD)
- A separate 16-bit integer, called 'record_fail' keeps track of which pixel failure categories      were checked (so we can distinguish cases where all pixels pass a category, or if that selection      wasn't applied.
- At the end of the analysis, a function is called ('count_pixels') which summarizes the number of pixels      failing each category.
- All of the pixel failure categories are treated the same, except for: DISCONNECTED_BUMPS_ZERO_BIAS_SCAN,       DISCONNECTED_BUMPS_XTALK_SCAN, and DISCONNECTED_BUMPS_SOURCE_SCAN. These categories are simply used for       diagnostic purposes, but pixels failing them do not count towards the total number of failed pixels.       Instead, a combined algorithm (DISCONNECTED_PIXELS) is used instead.
- Electrical pixel failures and disconnected failures are treated somewhat separately until the end of       the analysis, where they are combined into a single 'FAILING_PIXELS' category

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.VCAL_CALIBRATION.main
documentation:
Performs the VCal calibration.

It produces several diagnostic plots and an output file with the VCal calibration slope and offset.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.update_chip_config.main
documentation:
This script reads the analysis test type and update the corresponding parameters
in the chip config.

    Run this command after each analysis.

classes.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.update_chip_config.WriteConfig
documentation:
This class converts a parameter in a chip config to a given value.

- `in_path` must be the path to the output directory of the analysis.
- `config_path` must be the path to the directory ofchip config file in Yarr.
- if `permodule` enabled, the paths provided much be the path of the correct directory.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.ANALOG_READBACK.plot_stuff_vs_trim
documentation: # plot anything else against trim, no fit or target value

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.ANALOG_READBACK.main
documentation:
Performs the Analog Readback.

It produces an output file with the calculated internal biases, temperature from the internal and external temperature sensor, and VDDA/VDDD vs Trim, including diagnostic plots with slope and offset.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.IV_MEASURE.main
documentation:
Analyses sensor leakage current vs voltage measurement.

It produces an output file with several key parameters: breakdown voltage,
leakage current at operation voltage (depletion voltage + 20/50V for
3D/planar sensor), whether breakdown was observed and the absolute maximum
measured bias voltage.  Note that raw measurement data will be plotted and
uploaded onto the production database, which uses the absolute bias voltage
and leakage current regardless of the polarity. All currents will be
converted to uA.

If the depletion voltage if the sensor is unknown, please do not supply
anything to `--vdepl`. In this case either a value from the database or a
default value will be used.

One analysis criterion is the change wrt the bare module stage. For this,
an additional input file is required which provides the reference bare
module IV with up to 3 bare modules (triplets) in the format below. This is
generated in localDB. If none is supplied, the analysis will run but the
module will not pass.

??? note "Reference bare module IV format"

    ```json
    {
      'target_component' : <MODULE_SN>,
      'target_stage' : <MODULE_STAGE>,
      'reference_IVs' : [
        { 'component' : <SENSOR_TILE_SN>,
          'stage' : <bare module stage>,
          'Vbd' : <VALUE>,
          'Vfd' : <VALUE>,
          'temperature' : <VALUE>,
          'IV_ARRAY' : { "voltage" : [ array ], "current" : [array], "temperature": [array] }
        },
        { 'component' : <SENSOR_TILE_SN>,
          'stage' : <bare module stage>,
          'Vbd' : <VALUE>,
          'Vfd' : <VALUE>,
          'temperature' : <VALUE>,
          'IV_ARRAY' : { "voltage" : [ array ], "current" : [array], "temperature": [array] }
        },
        { 'component' : <SENSOR_TILE_SN>,
          'stage' : <bare module stage>,
          'Vbd' : <VALUE>,
          'Vfd' : <VALUE>,
          'temperature' : <VALUE>,
          'IV_ARRAY' : { "time": [array], "voltage" : [ array ], "current" : [array], "sigma current": [array], "temperature": [array], "humidity": [array] }
        }
      ]
    }
    ```

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.FLATNESS.main
documentation: This analysis script performs QC criteria checks on the flatness measurement.

modules.name: module_qc_analysis_tools._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.TUNING.main
documentation:
Analyzes the tuning performance from YARR scans.

It produces an output file with key parameters (threshold before/after tuning, ...). Note that the YARR scans to be used in the analysis should be identified with [`mqat analysis load-yarr-scans`](#mqat-config-load-yarr-scans).

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.cli.SLDO.main
documentation:
Analyses the SLDO curve.

It produces several diagnostic plots and an output file with several parameters extracted from the SLDO curves.

modules.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.coth
documentation: # # begin IV stuff
# # numbers from specification documents:
# # - 3D sensor QA/QC document: AT2-IP-QC-0003
# # - Planar sensor QC/QC document: AT2-IP-QC-0004
# # - Module spec: AT2-IP-ES-0009

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.normalise_current
documentation:
Function to convert leakage current measured at one temperature (deg C) to leakage current at a target temperature (deg C).
Note that this function only scales the bulk current and does not take any surface or edge effect into account. Thus in order to maintain accuracy, the input temperature should be similar to the target temperature.

current: can be a number or a list
orig_temp: can be a number or a list

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.module_sensor_area
documentation: Function to return sensor area in a module (cm^2) depending on the layer

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.sensor_tile_area
documentation: Function to return sensor tile area (cm^2) depending on the sensor type

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.depletion_voltage_threshold
documentation: Function to return the min depletion voltage (V) depending on the sensor type, same for sensor tile, bare module and module

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.depletion_voltage_default
documentation: Function to return the default depletion voltage (V) of a sensor type

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.operation_voltage
documentation: Function to return the operation voltage (V) depending on the depletion voltage

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.find_breakdown_and_current
documentation: # input voltage array, (normalised) current array, Vdepl

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.current_threshold
documentation: Function to return maximum allowed leakage current (uA) per area (cm^2)

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.current_increase_threshold
documentation: Function to return the maximum allowed leakage current given the leakage current of the previous stage

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.update_iv_cuts
documentation: Function to update the QC cuts on the breakdown voltage depending on the depletion voltage

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.get_nominal_current
documentation: ### end IV stuff

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.get_key
documentation: Function to get key from value in muxMaps

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.analysis.perform_qc_analysis
documentation: Function takes as input test type (i.e. ADC_CALIBRATION), file containing QC selections,
and dictionary with results. Returns QC result (true/false), a numpy array of text to
be printed later into a table, and the same result dictionary it was passed, but with
the values rounded to the same precision as the QC selection they are compared to.

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.misc.linear_fit
documentation:
Linear fit with PyROOT

Args:
    x (array): the x values, input DACs for VCal calibration
    y (array): the y values, measured Vmux[int] for VCal calibration

Returns:
    result (tuple(float, float)): the offset and slope of the fitted line

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.misc.linear_fit_np
documentation:
Linear fit with NumPy. numpy.linalg.lstsq() is called.

Args:
    x (array): the x values, input DACs for VCal calibration
    y (array): the y values, measured Vmux[int] for VCal calibration

Returns:
    p1 (float): the slope of the fitted line
    p0 (float): the offset of the fitted line

modules.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_fail_bit
documentation: Given a pixel failure category, returns corresponding bit

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_name_from_bit
documentation: Given pixel failure bit, returns corresponding category name

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.set_bit
documentation: Sets bit as 1 in integer

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_loc_from_index
documentation: Given array of pixel indices, returns corresponding rows and columns of those pixels

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_index_from_loc
documentation: Given row and column of single pixel, returns corresponding index

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.read_json
documentation: Loads json file

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.format_coreCol_input
documentation: Uses global chip config to create a pixel map of pixels with core columns disabled. Inputs are chip config (json), chiptype (string, e.g. "RD53B"), failbit array (array of one int) which is just the params in the classification.json file. Returns the map and the number of disabled core columns

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.check_input_yarr_config
documentation: Schema check for json file containing all input YARR scans

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.check_input_yarr_data
documentation: Schema check for output data from YARR scan

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.format_pixel_input
documentation: Transforms pixel data into flattened array
Pixel data is formatted so 0th column is FE pad side, and orientation is so that pads are on top (looking top-down on module)

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.format_TDAC_input
documentation: Loads TDAC input from chip config and returns as flattened array

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.format_enable_input
documentation: Loads enable input from chip config returns as flattened array

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_pixel_index
documentation: Returns flattened array of pixel indices

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.check_test_params
documentation: Gets layer-specific selection, if necessary

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.check_test_method
documentation: Checks that pixel selection make sense with specified test method

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.check_record
documentation: Checks if specific pixel failure category was checked

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.get_result_arrays
documentation: Returns list of flattened arrays, where each array indicates which pixels passed/failed each failure category

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.count_pixels
documentation: Counts pixels failing each category (dependent / independent / integrated)

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.classify_pixels
documentation: Identify pixels in simple (not-custom-method) pixel failure category

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.identify_merged_pixels
documentation: Identifies merged pixels

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.identify_zerobias_pixels
documentation: Identifies pixels with low noise difference

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.identify_noxtalk_pixels
documentation: Identifies pixels without cross-talk in disconnected bump scan

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.identify_nosource_pixels
documentation: Identify pixels without enough hits in source scan

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.plot_1d_hist
documentation: Makes 1D histograms of tuning data

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.plot_1d_discbumps
documentation: Makes 1D histograms comparing 0-bias & disconnected bump scan, and source & disconnected bump scan data

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.plot_2d_discbumps
documentation: Makes 2D histogram of pixel map, comparing consistency of source scan and disconnected bump scan results

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.plot_2d_map
documentation: Makes 2D histogram of pixel map

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.identify_disc_pixels
documentation: Combines information from all disconnected bump methods to determine which pixels to count as disconnected

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.print_pixel_classification
documentation: Prints summary of pixel classification to terminal

functions.name: module_qc_analysis_tools.utils.classification.split_pixels
documentation: Split pixels based on their location in the chip
